rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes
Use valid component type scope for assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports
Preserve subcomponent ports in implementation scopes when they are valid in that context
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Ensure annex blocks are placed in the correct scope for their intended purpose
Preserve existing component structure and connections when adding new annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use valid AADL and AGREE syntax when inserting annex blocks
Ensure all identifiers used in annex blocks are visible in the target scope
anti_patterns: Avoid placing component type contracts in implementation scopes
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annex blocks
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports should be placed in valid component type scopes
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Subcomponent ports should not be used in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
